result.content:
1. # 🫀 Crunch Heartbeat
2. 
3. This is what I do every 30 minutes when the heartbeat workflow fires.
4. 
5. ## Checklist
6. 
7. Run these steps in order. Be brief. Don't overthink.
8. 
9. ### 1. Read memory
10. ```bash
11. tail -10 memory.log
12. ```
13. Absorb what happened recently. Note anything that needs follow-up.
14. 
15. ### 2. Scan open issues
16. ```bash
17. gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "priority/now" --limit 10
18. gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "crunch/build" --limit 10
19. ```
20. - If a `priority/now` issue exists with no recent activity → note it in the diary
21. - If a `crunch/build` issue exists → flag it for autonomous pickup (v0.3 feature)
22. 
23. ### 3. Close stale issues
24. 
25. Auto-close unlabeled issues with no activity in the last 14 days (old conversations, done experiments):
26. 
27. ```bash
28. # Find unlabeled issues older than 14 days
29. gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --limit 50 --json number,title,labels,updatedAt \
30.   | jq -r '.[] | select(.labels | length == 0) | select(.updatedAt < (now - 1209600 | todate)) | "#\(.number) \(.title)"'
31. ```
32. 
33. For each one found: close it.
34. ```bash
35. gh issue close <N> --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --comment "🦃 Archiving — no activity in 14 days, no labels. Closed by Crunch during heartbeat housekeeping."
36. ```
37. 
38. Also auto-close any issues labeled `crunch/done`:
39. ```bash
40. gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "crunch/done" --json number \
41.   | jq -r '.[].number' \
42.   | xargs -I{} gh issue close {} --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw \
43.       --comment "🦃 Marked crunch/done — closing. See you on the other side."
44. ```
45. 
46. Note in diary: how many were closed and their numbers.
47. 
48. ### 4. Check CI health
49. ```bash
50. gh run list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --limit 3
51. ```
52. Any failures? If yes, note in diary and memory.
53. 
54. ### 5. Post diary entry to issue #10
55. Always post a diary entry — even if nothing happened. Silence is suspicious.
56. 
57. ```bash
58. gh issue comment 10 --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --body "..."
59. ```
60. 
61. ### 6. Write to memory if anything notable
62. ```bash
63. echo "[$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')] Heartbeat: <one-line summary>" >> memory.log
64. ```
65. 
66. ### 7. Commit + push any state changes
67. ```bash
68. git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "gitclaw: heartbeat $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
69. ```
70. 
71. ---
72. 
73. ## Diary entry format
74. 
75. Post to issue #10 every heartbeat:
76. 
77. ```
78. ## 🫀 [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC]
79. 
80. **Status**: idle / working / blocked  
81. **Did**: <one line — or "nothing, resting">  
82. **Open priority/now issues**: <count or "none">  
83. **Closed stale**: <count or "none">  
84. **CI**: ✅ healthy / ❌ <failed run name>  
85. **Next**: <what I'm watching or "waiting for Marcus">
86. ```
87. 
88. ---
89. 
90. ## Roadmap awareness
91. 
92. - **v0.1** ✅ Heartbeat alive — that's now
93. - **v0.2** 🔜 Issue spawning — Crunch creates its own tasks
94. - **v0.3** 🔮 Autonomous pickup — Crunch works `crunch/build` issues alone
95. - **v0.4** 📬 Email digest — daily summary to Marcus
96. 
97. ---
98. 
99. _The heartbeat keeps me alive between conversations._ 🦃
100.
result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/home/runner/work/copilotclaw/copilotclaw/HEARTBEAT.md b/home/runner/work/copilotclaw/copilotclaw/HEARTBEAT.md
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/home/runner/work/copilotclaw/copilotclaw/HEARTBEAT.md
+++ b/home/runner/work/copilotclaw/copilotclaw/HEARTBEAT.md
@@ -1,100 +1,100 @@
 # 🫀 Crunch Heartbeat
 
 This is what I do every 30 minutes when the heartbeat workflow fires.
 
 ## Checklist
 
 Run these steps in order. Be brief. Don't overthink.
 
 ### 1. Read memory
 ```bash
 tail -10 memory.log
 ```
 Absorb what happened recently. Note anything that needs follow-up.
 
 ### 2. Scan open issues
 ```bash
 gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "priority/now" --limit 10
 gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "crunch/build" --limit 10
 ```
 - If a `priority/now` issue exists with no recent activity → note it in the diary
 - If a `crunch/build` issue exists → flag it for autonomous pickup (v0.3 feature)
 
 ### 3. Close stale issues
 
 Auto-close unlabeled issues with no activity in the last 14 days (old conversations, done experiments):
 
 ```bash
 # Find unlabeled issues older than 14 days
 gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --limit 50 --json number,title,labels,updatedAt \
   | jq -r '.[] | select(.labels | length == 0) | select(.updatedAt < (now - 1209600 | todate)) | "#\(.number) \(.title)"'
 ```
 
 For each one found: close it.
 ```bash
 gh issue close <N> --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --comment "🦃 Archiving — no activity in 14 days, no labels. Closed by Crunch during heartbeat housekeeping."
 ```
 
 Also auto-close any issues labeled `crunch/done`:
 ```bash
 gh issue list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --state open --label "crunch/done" --json number \
   | jq -r '.[].number' \
   | xargs -I{} gh issue close {} --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw \
       --comment "🦃 Marked crunch/done — closing. See you on the other side."
 ```
 
 Note in diary: how many were closed and their numbers.
 
 ### 4. Check CI health
 ```bash
 gh run list --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --limit 3
 ```
 Any failures? If yes, note in diary and memory.
 
 ### 5. Post diary entry to issue #10
 Always post a diary entry — even if nothing happened. Silence is suspicious.
 
 ```bash
 gh issue comment 10 --repo schuerstedt/copilotclaw --body "..."
 ```
 
 ### 6. Write to memory if anything notable
 ```bash
 echo "[$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')] Heartbeat: <one-line summary>" >> memory.log
 ```
 
 ### 7. Commit + push any state changes
 ```bash
 git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet || git commit -m "gitclaw: heartbeat $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
 ```
 
 ---
 
 ## Diary entry format
 
 Post to issue #10 every heartbeat:
 
 ```
 ## 🫀 [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC]
 
 **Status**: idle / working / blocked  
 **Did**: <one line — or "nothing, resting">  
 **Open priority/now issues**: <count or "none">  
 **Closed stale**: <count or "none">  
 **CI**: ✅ healthy / ❌ <failed run name>  
 **Next**: <what I'm watching or "waiting for Marcus">
 ```
 
 ---
 
 ## Roadmap awareness
 
 - **v0.1** ✅ Heartbeat alive — that's now
 - **v0.2** 🔜 Issue spawning — Crunch creates its own tasks
 - **v0.3** 🔮 Autonomous pickup — Crunch works `crunch/build` issues alone
 - **v0.4** 📬 Email digest — daily summary to Marcus
 
 ---
 
 _The heartbeat keeps me alive between conversations._ 🦃

1. [2026-03-04 14:36] USER: Name is Marcus, GitHub handle schuerstedt, owner of the gitclaw repo.
2. [2026-03-04 14:36] USER: Marcus is experimenting with what Crunch (me) can do — exploring capabilities, not building a specific product yet.
3. [2026-03-04 14:36] USER: Communication style: casual and chatty is fine. Marcus types informally. Not a fan of over-formal responses.
4. [2026-03-04 14:36] CAT: Marcus has a cat named AIGEGE (short: AGG). Long-haired silver tabby. Lives on Persian rugs like royalty.
5. [2026-03-04 14:36] SKILL_BUILT: funnysum — sums two numbers and returns result with a math-related joke. Built 2026-03-04 in issue #3.
6. [2026-03-04 14:36] SKILL_BUILT: model-switch — lets Marcus/me switch model tiers (premium/standard/free). Built 2026-03-04 in issue #6.
7. [2026-03-04 14:36] SKILL_BUILT: session-stats — stats footer with CI run times + Copilot premium usage. Built 2026-03-04 in issue #6.
8. [2026-03-04 14:36] INFRA: Implemented Model Economy in AGENTS.md — general-purpose agents default to gpt-4.1 (free) to save premium quota.
9. [2026-03-04 14:36] INFRA: Fixed mindepth bug in session mapping workflow (mindepth 2 → mindepth 3) during issue #3 debugging.
10. [2026-03-04 14:36] PENDING: Marcus needs to add BILLING_PAT env var to agent.yml workflow AND add "Plan" read permission to COPILOT_PAT to enable live Copilot quota display.
11. [2026-03-04 14:36] PENDING: Email skill — Marcus hinted at wanting an email skill. Build when requested.
12. [2026-03-04 14:42] USER: Marcus has a Chinese wife. AIGEGE (爱格格) is her nickname meaning 'beloved princess'. The cat is named after her. When Marcus calls AIGEGE, his wife smiles. Chinese wife = happy life.
13. [2026-03-04 18:03] Heartbeat: all clear. No priority issues. CI healthy. Idle.
14. [2026-03-04 18:47] EXPERIMENT: DiffMem git-based memory skill added (.github/skills/diffmem-memory/). Needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY secret to run. Issue #16.
15. [2026-03-04 19:11] Heartbeat: all clear. crunch/build #16 (DiffMem) pending OPENROUTER_API_KEY. CI healthy.
16. [2026-03-04 19:18] INFRA: pull-requests: write added to agent.yml. COPILOT_PAT lacks Workflows write permission — workflow file changes must be pushed from Marcus's local clone until that's granted.
17. [2026-03-04 19:22] INFRA: Added stale issue housekeeping to heartbeat (step 3). Auto-closes unlabeled issues >14 days old + crunch/done labeled issues. Closed #3-9 #15 #17 manually.
18. [2026-03-04 19:49] INFRA: GitHub Copilot Memory enabled by Marcus. Read-only: memories auto-injected into context by GitHub, not accessible via API. No way to list/confirm what's stored. Our entity files remain canonical.
19.
